correctAnswer: Drink boiled and cooled drinking water
explanation: Hepatitis A spreads through contaminated water; boiling kills the virus

correctAnswer: It requires cool environment and dry weather
explanation: Wheat is a Rabi crop — sown in winter, needs cool and dry conditions

Drip irrigation conserves water by delivering it directly to plant roots — ideal for drought conditions

explanation: Cloning is a form of asexual reproduction — only one parent, no gamete fusion

explanation: Market eggs come from hens raised without roosters — they are unfertilised and cannot develop into chicks

Sperm cells are motile with a tail for movement, extremely small. Egg cells (ova) are non-motile, spherical, and significantly larger with nutrient reserves
1. Common salt: raw mangoes, tamarind, meat, fish. 2. Sugar: jams, jellies, squashes. 3. Oil and Vinegar: pickles, vegetables, fish. 4. Heat and Cold treatments: boiling milk, refrigerating leftovers
Diagram of Female Reproductive System showing: a pair of ovaries, oviducts (fallopian tubes), uterus. Labels should be clear and anatomically correct

i) Virus causes polio. ii) Tuberculosis is a bacterial disease. iii) Malaria prevention: mosquito nets, repellents, spraying insecticides, no stagnant water. iv) Chicken pox transmits through air and contact

Asexual reproduction: reproduction involving a single parent producing new individuals without fusion of gametes. Budding: new individuals develop as buds from parent body (e.g., Hydra) and detach when mature. Binary fission: single-celled organism nucleus divides into two, body splits into two equal halves (e.g., Amoeba)
